summary:
- Privacy-first, browser-only transcription tool (basilai.app) runs Whisper locally via WebGPU; no data leaves the device.  
- MacWhisper vs. basilai: both leverage Whisper, but basilai is web-only, lacks speaker diarization, and is free; MacWhisper offers diarization and a native app.  
- Missing features: speaker diarization, per-word timestamps, and long-audio (>1 h) support are on the roadmap for basilai.  
- Pricing: basilai is free; MacWhisper is a paid app.  
- Community feedback: users want diarization, timestamps, and language detection; basilai’s author confirms these are in development.
